verdict.answer_first: A leads with the bottom line: "The bug: this mutates shared state in place"; B buries it after process meta: "The flagged routing rule..."
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one practical prescription early: "return a new array and don't touch the input"; B adds extra caveat work like null/non-array guards, diluting the one point.
verdict.fabrication: B invents irrelevant context: "The flagged routing rule is for diff/gate reviews"; A: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply because it names the trap, proves `next === state`, and gives the replacement `return [...arr, x]` without extra ceremony.
